records.direction: Replace blockwise R updates with a full-batch multiplicative (mirror-descent-style) update and theta annealing.
Replaced block-wise stochastic R updates with a full-batch mirror-descent/exponentiated-gradient simplex update and recomputed O and E.
Replaced blockwise R updates with global mirror-descent exponentiated-gradient updates and delta_R convergence
Replace blockwise stochastic R updates with a global mirror-descent (exponentiated-gradient) update using step size.
Add a continuation/annealing schedule that scales theta (and exposes sigma_eff) across Harmony iterations.
Replace blockwise multiplicative diversity update with an entropic Sinkhorn/IPFP projection enforcing per-cluster batch-balance on R.
Introduce vMF mixture EM parameters and objective, replacing k-means-style updates with vMF-based responsibilities

records.direction: Add adaptive per-cluster sigma and replace K independent ridge regressions with one global weighted ridge.
Replaced IPFP hard batch-balance projection with soft-penalty fixed-point softmax update and damping.
Replace per-cluster diversity penalty with a KL-to-target log-ratio penalty mapped to cells via Phi.
Introduce deterministic annealing for sigma and theta and call a low-rank shared correction instead of ridge
Adaptive per-cluster assignment temperature (sigma) is tuned to target a perplexity/entropy schedule during clustering.
records.error_message:
Metric computation failed: Input X contains NaN.
NearestNeighbors does not accept missing values encoded as NaN natively. For supervised learning, you might want to consider sklearn.ensemble.HistGradientBoostingClassifier and Regressor which accept missing values encoded as NaNs natively. Alternatively, it is possible to preprocess the data, for instance by using an imputer transformer in a pipeline or drop samples with missing values. See https://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/impute.html You can find a list of all estimators that handle NaN values at the following page: https://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/impute.html#estimators-that-handle-nan-values

records.direction: Apply deterministic annealing in update_R: softmax(logits / T) with decreasing T and hard one-hot assignments.
Replace sequential per-cluster corrections with a joint estimation of W_k and add trust-region penalty.

Added Dirichlet KL-to-target batch-penalty in update_R and trust-region scaling for correction.

Added adaptive per-cluster sigma updates and use of clamped sigma in responsibility computations.
Replace blockwise responsibility updates with a global Sinkhorn-style entropic scaling enforcing batch-balanced responsibilities.
